## Verses (40)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | I made an agreement with my eyes; how then might my eyes be looking on a virgin? | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | For what is God's reward from on high, or the heritage given by the Ruler of all from heaven? | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | Is it not trouble for the sinner, and destruction for the evil-doers? | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Does he not see my ways, and are not my steps all numbered? | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | If I have gone in false ways, or my foot has been quick in working deceit; | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | (Let me be measured in upright scales, and let God see my righteousness:) | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | If my steps have been turned out of the way, or if my heart went after my eyes, or if the property of another is in my hands; | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Let me put seed in the earth for another to have the fruit of it, and let my produce be uprooted. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | If my heart went after another man's wife, or if I was waiting secretly at my neighbour's door; | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Then let my wife give pleasure to another man and let others make use of her body. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | For that would be a crime; it would be an act for which punishment would be measured out by the judges: | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | It would be a fire burning even to destruction, and taking away all my produce. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | If I did wrong in the cause of my man-servant, or my woman-servant, when they went to law with me; | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | What then will I do when God comes as my judge? and what answer may I give to his questions? | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Did not God make him as well as me? did he not give us life in our mothers' bodies? | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | If I kept back the desire of the poor; if the widow's eye was looking for help to no purpose; | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | If I kept my food for myself, and did not give some of it to the child with no father; | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | (For I was cared for by God as by a father from my earliest days; he was my guide from the body of my mother;) | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | If I saw one near to death for need of clothing, and that the poor had nothing covering him; | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | If his back did not give me a blessing, and the wool of my sheep did not make him warm; | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | If my hand had been lifted up against him who had done no wrong, when I saw that I was supported by the judges; | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | May my arm be pulled from my body, and be broken from its base. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | For the fear of God kept me back, and because of his power I might not do such things. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | If I made gold my hope, or if I ever said to the best gold, I have put my faith in you; | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | If I was glad because my wealth was great, and because my hand had got together a great store; | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | If, when I saw the sun shining, and the moon moving on its bright way, | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | A secret feeling of worship came into my heart, and my hand gave kisses from my mouth; | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | That would have been another sin to be rewarded with punishment by the judges; for I would have been false to God on high. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | If I was glad at the trouble of my hater, and gave cries of joy when evil overtook him; | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | (For I did not let my mouth give way to sin, in putting a curse on his life;) | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
| [31](BBE.md#v31) | If the men of my tent did not say, Who has not had full measure of his meat? | [WEB](WEB.md#v31) · [KJV](KJV.md#v31) · [ASV](ASV.md#v31) |
| [32](BBE.md#v32) | The traveller did not take his night's rest in the street, and my doors were open to anyone on a journey; | [WEB](WEB.md#v32) · [KJV](KJV.md#v32) · [ASV](ASV.md#v32) |
| [33](BBE.md#v33) | If I kept my evil doings covered, and my sin in the secret of my breast, | [WEB](WEB.md#v33) · [KJV](KJV.md#v33) · [ASV](ASV.md#v33) |
| [34](BBE.md#v34) | For fear of the great body of people, or for fear that families might make sport of me, so that I kept quiet, and did not go out of my door; | [WEB](WEB.md#v34) · [KJV](KJV.md#v34) · [ASV](ASV.md#v34) |
| [35](BBE.md#v35) | If only God would give ear to me, and the Ruler of all would give me an answer! or if what he has against me had been put in writing! | [WEB](WEB.md#v35) · [KJV](KJV.md#v35) · [ASV](ASV.md#v35) |
| [36](BBE.md#v36) | Truly I would take up the book in my hands; it would be to me as a crown; | [WEB](WEB.md#v36) · [KJV](KJV.md#v36) · [ASV](ASV.md#v36) |
| [37](BBE.md#v37) | I would make clear the number of my steps, I would put it before him like a prince! The words of Job are ended. | [WEB](WEB.md#v37) · [KJV](KJV.md#v37) · [ASV](ASV.md#v37) |
| [38](BBE.md#v38) | If my land has made an outcry against me, or the ploughed earth has been in sorrow; | [WEB](WEB.md#v38) · [KJV](KJV.md#v38) · [ASV](ASV.md#v38) |
| [39](BBE.md#v39) | If I have taken its produce without payment, causing the death of its owners; | [WEB](WEB.md#v39) · [KJV](KJV.md#v39) · [ASV](ASV.md#v39) |
| [40](BBE.md#v40) | Then in place of grain let thorns come up, and in place of barley evil-smelling plants. | [WEB](WEB.md#v40) · [KJV](KJV.md#v40) · [ASV](ASV.md#v40) |
